title: Trichoon roxburghii
intro: Trichoon roxburghii, also known as "Tongasali" or "Yarsagumba", is a rare and unique plant species found in the Himalayan region, belonging to the Orobanchaceae family of parasitic plants. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including its ability to improve respiratory function, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system, as well as its potential anti-cancer properties. Trichoon roxburghii contains various bioactive constituents, including alkaloids, phenolic compounds, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Trichoon roxburghii include tablets, capsules, and infusions, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory issues and other health conditions.

It is a deciduous shrub or small tree, with a range of characteristics that distinguish it from other plants in its family.
